title: Outcome tracking: post-delivery defect and feedback log
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual measures success by outcomes delivered to beneficiaries, not tasks merged. After the first delivery (delivery-001), a post-delivery defect and feedback log is required to track adoption status, critical defects (target 0), readability outcomes, and partner/beneficiary usefulness feedback. This feeds the success metrics in PLAN.md and supports the withdraw-first correction policy.
objective: Set up and maintain a per-language outcome tracking log recording: adoption status, post-delivery critical defects (meaning-changing errors, negation/urgency errors, advice creep — target 0), readability outcomes, and partner/beneficiary usefulness feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/outcome-log.md (or outcome-log.yaml) is created with a per-language section recording: adoption status (adopted or not yet adopted), post-delivery critical defects log (date, term, defect description, severity, resolution), readability outcomes, and partner/beneficiary usefulness feedback
Log distinguishes critical defects (meaning-changing, negation/urgency error, advice creep) from non-critical issues; critical defect target is 0
Log is structured to feed the success metrics table in PLAN.md
A procedure is noted for handling post-delivery inaccuracies: withdraw first, correct, re-review, then re-publish
output: docs/outcome-log.md (or outcome-log.yaml) with per-language outcome tracking structure and initial entry after delivery-001
